title: Pin behavior with characterization tests first
description: Capture current observable behavior in tests before refactoring legacy code that lacks coverage, so regressions surface immediately.
body: Use when refactoring code that has little or no existing test coverage. Do not restructure untested legacy code on the assumption you understand it. Actions: write characterization tests that record the code's actual current outputs (including quirks) for representative inputs, run them green against the unchanged code, then refactor and re-run to confirm identical outputs. Validation signals include the characterization suite passing both before and after with no output diff. Risk boundary: refactoring without a behavior net risks silent regressions in edge cases; if behavior cannot be pinned, narrow the change or escalate rather than refactoring blind.
